name: Pumpkin Cheesecake with Maple/Bourbon Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups Fine gingersnap crumbs (about 1/2 lb. gingersnaps)
1/2 pound Cream cheese, at room temperature
1 cup Unsweetened pumpkin puree (fresh or canned, not pumpkin pie Filling)
recipeInstructions:
1. Lightly butter a 10x2 inch round cake pan.
2. Combine gingersnap crumbs, nuts, and butter in a bowl and mix well. Press mixture firmly into prepared pan, and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
3. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 C).
4. Make Filling: In a large bowl, beat cream cheese until smooth. Add sugars and spices, beating until light in texture (3-4 minutes). Add pumpkin puree and mix until just blended. Stir in eggs one at a time, scraping sides of the bowl after each addition. Stir in heavy cream.
5. Pour batter into crust, place cake pan in a roasting pan, and add hot water to roasting pan halfway up the sides of the cake pan. Bake in the center of the oven until cheesecake is firm to touch and slightly puffed (about 50 minutes).
6. Let cheesecake cool on a wire rack for about 45 minutes. Cover, and refrigerate for at least 4 hours.
7. When cheesecake is completely chilled, lower the pan into a hot water bath for 2-3 minutes to loosen the crust. Invert a large, flat plate on top of the cheesecake; invert the pan, and remove it. Put a second plate on the bottom of the cheesecake, and turn it right side up. Cut with a hot knife, and serve with maple/bourbon sauce.
8. Cheesecake will keep for 4-5 days in the refrigerator and 2 weeks in the freezer.
Maple-Bourbon Sauce:
1/4 cup Maple Syrup
3 tablespoons Bourbon
1 cup Light Cream
1/4 Vanilla Bean, split lengthwise
3 Large Egg Yolks
1/4 cup Sugar
3/4 teaspoon Cornstarch
1/3 cup Heavy Cream
Combine maple syrup and bourbon in a saucepan. Over medium heat, bring to a boil, and cook until reduced by 1/3 (about 10 minutes); set aside to cool.
Put light cream and vanilla bean in a large saucepan, and bring to a boil. Immediately remove from heat, and let the bean infuse the cream for about 10 minutes. Remove the bean, and scrape the pulp into the cream.
Whisk together egg yolks, sugar, and cornstarch. Stir in 1/2 cup cream and return the mixture to the saucepan with the rest of the cream. Stirring continuously, cook over low heat until thickened enough to coat the back of a wooden spoon (5-8 minutes).
Add reduced maple syrup and bourbon, and strain through a fine sieve into a bowl set in ice. Stir until completely chilled, then fold in whipped cream.
